006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.conversion;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020
021import java.lang.reflect.*;
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.*;
024import java.util.stream.*;
025
026/**
027 * An abstract {@link Converter} implementation that caches previously determined type conversions.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * Conversion functions are discovered lazily on the first call for a given input/output type pair and
031 * stored in a two-level {@link ConcurrentHashMap} for subsequent fast lookup.
032 *
033 * <p>
034 * Subclasses implement {@link #findConversion(Class, Class)} to provide the actual conversion logic.
035 * The result is cached so that reflection or other expensive discovery happens only once per type pair.
036 *
037 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
038 * <p class='bjava'>
039 *    <jk>public class</jk> MyConverter <jk>extends</jk> CachingConverter {
040 *
041 *       <ja>@Override</ja>
042 *       <jk>protected</jk> &lt;I,O&gt; Conversion&lt;I,O&gt; findConversion(Class&lt;I&gt; inType, Class&lt;O&gt; outType) {
043 *          <jk>if</jk> (inType == String.<jk>class</jk> &amp;&amp; outType == Integer.<jk>class</jk>)
044 *             <jk>return</jk> (Conversion&lt;I,O&gt;) (Conversion&lt;String,Integer&gt;) (<jv>s</jv>, <jv>memberOf</jv>, <jv>session</jv>, <jv>args</jv>) -&gt; Integer.<jsm>valueOf</jsm>(<jv>s</jv>);
045 *          <jk>return null</jk>;
046 *       }
047 *    }
048 * </p>
049 *
050 * <h5 class='section'>Thread Safety:</h5>
051 * <p>
052 * This class is thread-safe. The cache uses {@link ConcurrentHashMap} for safe concurrent access.
053 * </p>
054 */
055public abstract class CachingConverter implements Converter {
056
057   // Sentinel stored in the cache when findConversion() returns null.
058   // ConcurrentHashMap does not permit null values, so we cannot store null directly.
059   // Using this sentinel avoids re-invoking findConversion() for unconvertable type pairs.
060   private static final Conversion<?,?> NO_CONVERSION = (in, memberOf, session, args) -> null; // HTT: sentinel body is never invoked; ConcurrentHashMap prohibits null values so we use this placeholder
061
062   /**
063    * Returns the appropriate default value for a null input to the given target type.
064    *
065    * <ul>
066    *    <li>For {@link Optional}: returns {@link Optional#empty()}, or {@code Optional.of(Optional.empty())} for
067    *       nested optional types such as {@code Optional<Optional<Integer>>}.
068    *    <li>For primitives: returns the JVM zero/false default (e.g. {@code 0} for {@code int}).
069    *    <li>For all other types: returns {@code null}.
070    * </ul>
071    *
072    * @param type The target type.
073    * @param args The generic type arguments (e.g. element type for {@link Optional}).
074    * @param <T> The target type.
075    * @return The appropriate null default.
076    */
077   @SuppressWarnings({
078      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: type parameter is verified at construction.
079   })
080   static <T> T nullDefault(Class<T> type, Class<?>... args) {
081      if (type == Optional.class) {
082         if (args.length > 0)
083            return (T) opt(nullDefault(args[0], Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 1, args.length)));
084         return (T) opte();
085      }
086      if (type == OptionalInt.class)
087         return (T) OptionalInt.empty();
088      if (type == OptionalLong.class)
089         return (T) OptionalLong.empty();
090      if (type == OptionalDouble.class)
091         return (T) OptionalDouble.empty();
092      return type.isPrimitive() ? primitiveDefault(type) : null;
093   }
094
095   /**
096    * Returns the JVM default value for a primitive type, or <jk>null</jk> if the type is not primitive.
097    *
098    * <p>
099    * This is used to satisfy {@link #to} when the input is <jk>null</jk> and the target type is a
100    * non-boxed primitive (e.g. {@code int.class}), where returning <jk>null</jk> would be invalid.
101    *
102    * @param type The target type.
103    * @param <T> The target type.
104    * @return The JVM zero/false default, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code type} is not a primitive.
105    */
106   @SuppressWarnings({
107      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: type parameter is verified at construction.
108   })
109   static <T> T primitiveDefault(Class<T> type) {
110      if (type == Integer.TYPE)   return (T) Integer.valueOf(0);
111      if (type == Long.TYPE)      return (T) Long.valueOf(0L);
112      if (type == Double.TYPE)    return (T) Double.valueOf(0.0d);
113      if (type == Float.TYPE)     return (T) Float.valueOf(0.0f);
114      if (type == Boolean.TYPE)   return (T) Boolean.FALSE;
115      if (type == Short.TYPE)     return (T) Short.valueOf((short)0);
116      if (type == Byte.TYPE)      return (T) Byte.valueOf((byte)0);
117      if (type == Character.TYPE) return (T) Character.valueOf('\0');
118      return null; // HTT: no other primitive types exist in Java
119   }
120
121   // Two-level cache: input type -> output type -> conversion function (or NO_CONVERSION sentinel).
122   private final Map<Class<?>, Map<Class<?>, Conversion<?,?>>> conversions = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
123
124   /**
125    * Finds a conversion function from the specified input type to the specified output type.
126    *
127    * <p>
128    * This method is called lazily the first time a conversion between a given type pair is requested.
129    * The result is cached so subsequent calls for the same type pair bypass this method entirely.
130    * When this method returns <jk>null</jk>, a sentinel is stored so that unconvertable type pairs
131    * are also only evaluated once.
132    *
133    * <p>
134    * Implementations should return <jk>null</jk> if no conversion is possible for the given types,
135    * which will cause {@link #to(Object, Class)} and {@link #to(Object, Type, Type...)} to throw
136    * {@link InvalidConversionException} for that type pair.
137    *
138    * @param <I> The input type.
139    * @param <O> The output type.
140    * @param inType The runtime class of the input object.
141    * @param outType The target output class.
142    * @return A {@link Conversion} function, or <jk>null</jk> if no conversion is available.
143    */
144   protected abstract <I, O> Conversion<I, O> findConversion(Class<I> inType, Class<O> outType);
145
146   @SuppressWarnings({
147      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: type parameter is verified at construction.
148   })
149   private <I, O> Conversion<I, O> lookupConversion(Class<I> inType, Class<O> outType) {
150      var fn = conversions
151         .computeIfAbsent(inType, k -> new ConcurrentHashMap<>())
152         .computeIfAbsent(outType, k -> {
153            var found = findConversion(inType, outType);
154            return found != null ? found : NO_CONVERSION;
155         });
156      return fn == NO_CONVERSION ? null : (Conversion<I, O>) fn;
157   }
158
159   @Override
160   public boolean canConvert(Class<?> inType, Class<?> outType) {
161      if (inType == outType)
162         return true;
163      return lookupConversion(inType, outType) != null;
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Converts the specified object to the specified type.
168    *
169    * <p>
170    * On the first call for a given input/output type pair, {@link #findConversion(Class, Class)} is
171    * invoked and the result is cached. Subsequent calls use the cached function directly.
172    *
173    * @param o The object to convert. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
174    * @param type The target type.
175    * @param <T> The target type.
176    * @return The converted object, or the primitive zero-value if the input is <jk>null</jk> and the target
177    *    is a primitive type, or <jk>null</jk> otherwise.
178    * @throws InvalidConversionException If no conversion path exists from the input type to the target type.
179    */
180   @Override
181   @SuppressWarnings({
182      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: type parameter is verified at construction.
183   })
184   public <T> T to(Object o, Class<T> type) {
185      if (o == null)
186         return type.isPrimitive() ? primitiveDefault(type) : null;
187      var inType = o.getClass();
188      if (inType == type)
189         return (T) o;
190      var fn = (Conversion<Object, T>) lookupConversion(inType, type);
191      if (fn == null)
192         throw new InvalidConversionException(inType, type);
193      return fn.to(o, null, (ConverterSession)null);
194   }
195
196   /**
197    * Converts the specified object to the specified parameterized type.
198    *
199    * <p>
200    * The raw class is extracted from {@code mainType} (supporting both {@link Class} and
201    * {@link ParameterizedType} values). Type arguments are extracted from {@code args} the same way
202    * and passed through to the cached {@link Conversion} function at call time.
203    *
204    * <p>
205    * The cache is keyed on the raw input and output classes only. The {@code args} are not part of
206    * the cache key — a single cached {@link Conversion} handles all parameterizations of a given
207    * output type.
208    *
209    * @param o The object to convert. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
210    * @param mainType The target type. May be a {@link Class} or {@link ParameterizedType}.
211    * @param args The type arguments of the target type (e.g. element type for collections).
212    * @param <T> The target type.
213    * @return The converted object, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
214    * @throws InvalidConversionException If no conversion path exists from the input type to the target type.
215    */
216   @Override
217   @SuppressWarnings({
218      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: type parameter is verified at construction.
219   })
220   public <T> T to(Object o, Type mainType, Type... args) {
221      var rawType = (Class<T>) (mainType instanceof ParameterizedType pt ? pt.getRawType() : (Class<?>) mainType);
222      var argClasses = Stream.of(args)
223         .map(t -> (Class<?>) (t instanceof ParameterizedType pt2 ? pt2.getRawType() : t))
224         .toArray(Class[]::new);
225      if (o == null)
226         return nullDefault(rawType, argClasses);
227      var inType = o.getClass();
228      var fn = (Conversion<Object, T>) lookupConversion(inType, rawType);
229      if (fn == null)
230         throw new InvalidConversionException(inType, rawType);
231      return fn.to(o, null, (ConverterSession)null, argClasses);
232   }
233
234   /**
235    * Converts the specified object to the specified type, using the given outer instance and converter session.
236    *
237    * <p>
238    * The session is forwarded to the cached {@link Conversion} function so conversion lambdas can access
239    * contextual objects such as {@link java.util.TimeZone} or {@link java.util.Locale}.
240    *
241    * @param o The object to convert. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
242    * @param memberOf The outer instance for non-static inner class construction, or <jk>null</jk>.
243    * @param session The converter session providing contextual objects, or <jk>null</jk>.
244    * @param type The target type.
245    * @param <T> The target type.
246    * @return The converted object, or the primitive zero-value if the input is <jk>null</jk> and the target
247    *    is a primitive type, or <jk>null</jk> otherwise.
248    * @throws InvalidConversionException If no conversion path exists from the input type to the target type.
249    */
250   @Override
251   @SuppressWarnings({
252      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: type parameter is verified at construction.
253   })
254   public <T> T to(Object o, Object memberOf, ConverterSession session, Class<T> type) {
255      if (o == null)
256         return nullDefault(type);
257      var inType = o.getClass();
258      if (inType == type)
259         return (T) o;
260      var fn = (Conversion<Object, T>) lookupConversion(inType, type);
261      if (fn == null)
262         throw new InvalidConversionException(inType, type);
263      return fn.to(o, memberOf, session);
264   }
265
266   /**
267    * Converts the specified object to the specified parameterized type, using the given outer instance and converter session.
268    *
269    * <p>
270    * The session is forwarded to the cached {@link Conversion} function so conversion lambdas can access
271    * contextual objects such as {@link java.util.TimeZone} or {@link java.util.Locale}.
272    *
273    * @param o The object to convert. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
274    * @param memberOf The outer instance for non-static inner class construction, or <jk>null</jk>.
275    * @param session The converter session providing contextual objects, or <jk>null</jk>.
276    * @param mainType The target type. May be a {@link Class} or {@link ParameterizedType}.
277    * @param args The type arguments of the target type (e.g. element type for collections).
278    * @param <T> The target type.
279    * @return The converted object, or <jk>null</jk> if the input is <jk>null</jk>.
280    * @throws InvalidConversionException If no conversion path exists from the input type to the target type.
281    */
282   @Override
283   @SuppressWarnings({
284      "unchecked" // Cast is safe: type parameter is verified at construction.
285   })
286   public <T> T to(Object o, Object memberOf, ConverterSession session, Type mainType, Type... args) {
287      var rawType = (Class<T>) (mainType instanceof ParameterizedType pt ? pt.getRawType() : (Class<?>) mainType);
288      var argClasses = Stream.of(args)
289         .map(t -> (Class<?>) (t instanceof ParameterizedType pt2 ? pt2.getRawType() : t))
290         .toArray(Class[]::new);
291      if (o == null)
292         return nullDefault(rawType, argClasses);
293      var inType = o.getClass();
294      var fn = (Conversion<Object, T>) lookupConversion(inType, rawType);
295      if (fn == null)
296         throw new InvalidConversionException(inType, rawType);
297      var result = fn.to(o, memberOf, session, argClasses);
298      if (result == null && rawType.isPrimitive())
299         return primitiveDefault(rawType);
300      return result;
301   }
302}
